title: lithocardium reticulatum
intro: Lithocardium reticulatum, also known as Digitalis purpurea or Foxglove, is a flowering plant native to Europe and is a member of the Plantaginaceae family. The medicinal properties of Lithocardium reticulatum lie in its leaves, which contain glycosides that have a positive inotropic effect on the heart, increasing the force of contraction and helping to treat conditions such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. The health benefits of Lithocardium reticulatum include improved cardiovascular health, increased cardiac output, and enhanced overall circulation, making it a valuable remedy for heart-related issues. Lithocardium reticulatum can be prepared as a tincture, capsule, or dried leaf, and is often used in herbal medicine to support heart health and treat conditions such as arrhythmias and heart failure.
